summary: Good audience recording of a solid Spring‑92 show with standout Touch of Grey and Music Never Stopped, though the second set falls a bit short.
review: The three reviewers agree this is one of the better captures of the Spring ’92 run – an audience tape that’s not pristine but listenable. The first set shines with up‑tempo crowd‑pleasers like Touch of Grey, Black Throated Wind, Loose Lucy and a strong Music Never Stopped jam, while the second set is considered weaker. Jerry’s horn‑solo on Music is praised despite a later guitar‑tone volume issue, and Vince’s keyboard work is noted as repetitive.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally praised (70% weight) with a few criticisms of the second set; recording is deemed decent but not high‑fidelity (30% weight). The mix yields an overall solid 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Not the highest fidelity tape, but considered the best sounding capture of this evening; some listeners note a slightly muffled guitar tone and repetitive keyboard chords.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, high‑energy first set packed with up‑tempo crowd pleasers; the second set doesn’t quite match the excitement of the opener but remains an enjoyable night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Great horn solo on Music, later trad guitar tone suffers from drive/volume issue, but he twirls licks throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s repetitive chord work is noted; Hornsby’s absence is mentioned as a turning point for the tour.
